title: Final-stop window-opening indicators
summary: Distinguish cars already inside a fresh-tyre finish window from cars for which that window becomes newly feasible on the next lap.
rationale: The run repeatedly explored window-entry timing, and it is a target-aligned boundary condition for predicting next-lap stops. The signal is more specific than general feasibility because teams often pit when the final stint first becomes viable.
implementation_hint: For each slick compound, compute whether it cannot finish if stopping now but can finish if stopping next lap, plus an opening-count aggregate, softest-opening ordinal, and closest-opening-boundary margin. Test this as an addition to the option-richness block and ablate per-compound flags versus compact aggregates.
expected_effect: Small AUC gain with medium confidence; strongest expected on late-race boundary rows.
risk: Implementation bugs occurred when boolean opening flags were converted to integers before reuse. Keep intermediate conditions boolean until all selection or masking operations are complete.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
